G, title II, § 228(c)128 Stat. 2491Dec. 16, 2014“In the case of research, evaluations, and national studies funded under section 413(h)(1) of the Social Security Act [], no funds shall be appropriated under that section for fiscal year 2015 or any fiscal year thereafter.”42 U.S.C. 613(h)(1)

Coordination of Substance Abuse and Child Protection Services, , , required the Secretary of Health and Human Services, based on information from the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration and the Administration for Children and Families in the Department of Health and Human Services, to submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report which described the extent and scope of the problem of substance abuse in the child welfare population, the types of services provided to such population, and the outcomes resulting from the provision of such services to such population, along with appropriate recommendations for legislative changes.Pub. L. 105–89, title IV, § 405111 Stat. 2135Nov. 19, 1997

GAO Study of Effect of Family Violence on Need for Public Assistance, , , directed the Comptroller General to conduct a study of the effect of family violence on the use of public assistance programs, and in particular the extent to which family violence prolongs or increases the need for public assistance, and to submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress within 1 year after .Pub. L. 105–33, title V, § 5001(i)111 Stat. 593Aug. 5, 1997Aug. 5, 1997

children.heading: Evaluation of the impacts of TANF
children.label: a Evaluation of the impacts of TANF
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct research on the effect of State programs funded under this part and any other State program funded with qualified State expenditures (as defined in ) on employment, self-sufficiency, child well-being, unmarried births, marriage, poverty, economic mobility, and other factors as determined by the Secretary.section 609(a)(7)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Evaluation of grants to improve child well-being by promoting healthy marriage and responsible fatherhood
children.label: b Evaluation of grants to improve child well-being by promoting healthy marriage and responsible fatherhood
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct research to determine the effects of the grants made under on child well-being, marriage, family stability, economic mobility, poverty, and other factors as determined by the Secretary.section 603(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Dissemination of information
children.label: c Dissemination of information
children.text: The Secretary shall, in consultation with States receiving funds provided under this part, develop methods of disseminating information on any research, evaluation, or study conducted under this section, including facilitating the sharing of information and best practices among States and localities.
the State submits to the Secretary a description of the proposed evaluation;
the Secretary determines that the design and approach of the proposed evaluation is rigorous and is likely to yield information that is credible and will be useful to other States; and
unless waived by the Secretary, the State contributes to the cost of the evaluation, from non-Federal sources, an amount equal to at least 25 percent of the cost of the proposed evaluation.

The Bureau of the Census shall implement or enhance household surveys of program participation, in consultation with the Secretary and the Bureau of Labor Statistics and made available to interested parties, to allow for the assessment of the outcomes of continued welfare reform on the economic and child well-being of low-income families with children, including those who received assistance or services from a State program funded under this part or any other State program funded with qualified State expenditures (as defined in ). The content of the surveys should include such information as may be necessary to examine the issues of unmarried childbearing, marriage, welfare dependency and compliance with work requirements, the beginning and ending of spells of assistance, work, earnings and employment stability, and the well-being of children.section 609(a)(7)(B)(i) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: address under reporting of the receipt of means-tested benefits and tax benefits for low-income individuals and families;

children.label: B
children.text: increase understanding of poverty spells and long-term poverty, including by facilitating the matching of information to better understand intergenerational poverty;

children.label: C
children.text: generate a better geographical understanding of poverty such as through State-based estimates and measures of neighborhood poverty;

children.label: D
children.text: increase understanding of the effects of means-tested benefits and tax benefits on the earnings and incomes of low-income families; and

children.label: E
children.text: improve how poverty and economic well-being are measured, including through the use of consumption measures, material deprivation measures, social exclusion measures, and economic and social mobility measures.

children.heading: Research and evaluation conducted under this section
children.label: f Research and evaluation conducted under this section
children.text: Research and evaluation conducted under this section designed to determine the effects of a program or policy (other than research conducted under subsection (e)) shall use experimental designs using random assignment or other reliable, evidence-based research methodologies that allow for the strongest possible causal inferences when random assignment is not feasible.

children.heading: Development of What Works Clearinghouse of Proven and Promising Approaches to Move Welfare Recipients into Work1So in original. The word “Projects” is used in text.1
children.label: g Development of What Works Clearinghouse of Proven and Promising Approaches to Move Welfare Recipients into Work1So in original. The word “Projects” is used in text.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor, shall develop a database (which shall be referred to as the “What Works Clearinghouse of Proven and Promising Projects to Move Welfare Recipients into Work”) of the projects that used a proven approach or a promising approach in moving welfare recipients into work, based on independent, rigorous evaluations of the projects. The database shall include a separate listing of projects that used a developmental approach in delivering services and a further separate listing of the projects with no or negative effects. The Secretary shall add to the What Works Clearinghouse of Proven and Promising Projects to Move Welfare Recipients into Work data about the projects that, based on an independent, well-conducted experimental evaluation of a program or project, using random assignment or other research methodologies that allow for the strongest possible causal inferences, have shown they are proven, promising, developmental, or ineffective approaches.

children.heading: Criteria for evidence of effectiveness of approach
children.label: 2 Criteria for evidence of effectiveness of approach
A
children.text: establish criteria for evidence of effectiveness; and

children.label: i
children.text: is transparent;

children.label: ii
children.text: is consistent across agencies;

children.label: iii
children.text: provides opportunity for public comment; and

children.label: iv
children.text: takes into account efforts of Federal agencies to identify and publicize effective interventions, including efforts at the Department of Health and Human Services, the Department of Education, and the Department of Justice.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Of the amount appropriated by for each fiscal year, 0.33 percent shall be available for research, technical assistance, and evaluation under this section.section 603(a)(1) of this title

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: 2 Allocation
children.text: Of the amount made available under paragraph (1) for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall make available $10,000,000 plus such additional amount as the Secretary deems necessary and appropriate, to carry out subsection (e).

children.heading: Baseline
children.label: 3 Baseline
children.text: The baseline established pursuant to for the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families Program shall be recorded by the Office of Management and Budget and the Congressional Budget Office at the level prior to any transfers recorded pursuant to .section 907 of title 2section 613(h) of this title
